title: Our New Puppy
summary: A child tells the story of the day her family got a new puppy and the things she learned about caring for a dog. She describes the excitement and the new responsibilities.
body: Last month, my family got a new puppy. We named him Biscuit because his fur is the colour of a biscuit. He is a golden colour and very fluffy. When we first brought him home, he was very small and a little scared. He hid under the sofa and did not come out for a long time. My dad put some food near the sofa, and slowly Biscuit came out to eat. After that, he started to explore the house. He sniffed everything with his little nose. I learned that puppies need food, water, and walks every day. My job is to fill his water bowl every morning. My mum taught me how to wash my hands after touching him. We take Biscuit for a walk in the park every afternoon. He loves to run and play in the grass. Now he sleeps on a special dog bed in my room. Having a puppy is a lot of work, but he makes our family very happy.

annotations.exampleEn: The puppy wagged its tail when it saw me.
The cat's fur was soft and warm.
She hugged the fluffy teddy bear.
The child was scared of the loud thunder.
The cat hid behind the curtain.
The children loved to explore the forest near their house.
The dog sniffed the ground and then ran into the bushes.
Always make sure your dog's water bowl is full.
Looking after a pet is a big responsibility.
We bought a special bed for the dog.
She slowly opened the door so she wouldn't wake anyone.
Keeping the garden tidy is a lot of work.

grammarNotes.title: Simple Past – Regular and Irregular Verbs
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Nhiều động từ trong bài dùng thì quá khứ đơn. Động từ thường thêm '-ed': name → named, learn → learned, start → started. Nhưng một số động từ bất quy tắc thay đổi hoàn toàn: get → got, bring → brought, hide → hid, come → came, put → put, teach → taught. Hãy học và ghi nhớ các động từ bất quy tắc này.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: This story uses many simple past verbs. Regular verbs add '-ed': name → named, learn → learned. Irregular verbs change form entirely: get → got, bring → brought, hide → hid, come → came, teach → taught. It is important to memorise irregular verb forms.

grammarNotes.title: Frequency Adverbs: every day, every morning, every afternoon
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Every day', 'every morning', 'every afternoon' là các cụm từ chỉ tần suất — chúng cho biết hành động xảy ra thường xuyên như thế nào. Ví dụ: 'I fill his water bowl every morning.' (Tôi đổ đầy bát nước cho nó mỗi sáng.) Chúng thường đứng ở cuối câu.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Every day', 'every morning', 'every afternoon' are time expressions that show how often something happens. They usually appear at the end of a sentence. Example: 'We take Biscuit for a walk every afternoon.' — this tells us the walk is a daily routine.

translationVi: Tháng trước, gia đình tôi đã có một chú chó con mới. Chúng tôi đặt tên nó là Biscuit vì bộ lông của nó có màu như chiếc bánh quy. Nó có màu vàng và rất mịn lông. Khi mới đưa nó về nhà, nó còn rất nhỏ và hơi sợ hãi. Nó trốn dưới chiếc ghế sofa và không chịu ra ngoài trong một thời gian dài. Bố tôi để một ít thức ăn gần ghế sofa, và từ từ Biscuit chui ra để ăn. Sau đó, nó bắt đầu khám phá ngôi nhà. Nó ngửi khắp nơi bằng cái mũi nhỏ xíu của mình. Tôi học được rằng chó con cần thức ăn, nước uống và được đi dạo mỗi ngày. Công việc của tôi là đổ đầy bát nước cho nó mỗi buổi sáng. Mẹ tôi dạy tôi cách rửa tay sau khi sờ vào nó. Chúng tôi đưa Biscuit đi dạo trong công viên mỗi buổi chiều. Nó rất thích chạy và chơi trên bãi cỏ. Bây giờ nó ngủ trên một chiếc giường chó đặc biệt trong phòng của tôi. Nuôi chó con rất nhiều việc, nhưng nó mang lại niềm vui rất lớn cho gia đình tôi.

questions.prompt: When did the family get the puppy?
questions.explanation: The story says 'Last month, my family got a new puppy.'
questions.hint: How long ago did the story happen?

questions.prompt: Why is the puppy named Biscuit?
questions.explanation: The story says 'We named him Biscuit because his fur is the colour of a biscuit.'

questions.prompt: How did Biscuit feel when he first came home?
questions.explanation: The story says 'he was very small and a little scared.'
questions.hint: What was Biscuit's mood when he arrived?

questions.explanation: The story says 'He hid under the sofa.'
questions.hint: What piece of furniture did Biscuit hide beneath?

questions.prompt: What did the dad do to encourage Biscuit to come out?
questions.explanation: The story says 'My dad put some food near the sofa, and slowly Biscuit came out to eat.'
questions.hint: What did Dad use to tempt Biscuit out of hiding?

questions.prompt: What did Biscuit do when he started to explore the house?
questions.explanation: The story says 'He sniffed everything with his little nose.'
questions.hint: What body part did Biscuit use to explore?

questions.prompt: What is the child's daily job?
questions.explanation: The story says 'My job is to fill his water bowl every morning.'
questions.hint: What task is specifically the child's responsibility?

questions.prompt: What did Mum teach the child?
options: How to wash hands after touching Biscuit
How to give Biscuit a bath
questions.explanation: The story says 'My mum taught me how to wash my hands after touching him.'
questions.hint: What hygiene lesson did Mum teach?

questions.prompt: When do they take Biscuit for a walk?
questions.explanation: The story says 'We take Biscuit for a walk in the park every afternoon.'
questions.hint: What time of day do they visit the park?

options: On a dog bed in the child's room
In a box in the kitchen
questions.explanation: The story says 'Now he sleeps on a special dog bed in my room.'

questions.prompt: What does Biscuit love to do in the park?
options: Run and play in the grass
questions.explanation: The story says 'He loves to run and play in the grass.'
questions.hint: What does Biscuit enjoy doing outdoors?

cloze.template: Last month, my family got a new {{b1}}. We named him Biscuit because his {{b2}} is the colour of a biscuit. He is a golden colour and very {{b3}}. When we first brought him home, he was very small and a little {{b4}}. He hid under the sofa and did not come out for a long time. My dad put some food near the sofa, and slowly Biscuit came out to {{b5}}. After that, he started to {{b6}} the house. He sniffed everything with his little nose. I learned that puppies need food, water, and walks every day. My job is to fill his water {{b7}} every morning. My mum taught me how to wash my hands after {{b8}} him. We take Biscuit for a walk in the park every {{b9}}. He loves to run and play in the grass. Now he sleeps on a special dog bed in my room. Having a puppy is a lot of {{b10}}, but he makes our family very happy.

blanks.explanation: The story is about a new 'puppy' — a baby dog.
Animals have 'fur' — the soft covering of hair on their bodies.
'Fluffy' describes something soft and light, like a puppy with thick soft fur.
The puppy 'hid' because he was 'scared' — afraid of the new place.
Dad put food near the sofa, so Biscuit came out to 'eat'.
'Explore' means to look around a new place to find out what is there.
A 'water bowl' is the round container used for a dog's drinking water.
The story says 'wash my hands after touching him' — an important hygiene habit.
The story says 'every afternoon' — the park walk is an afternoon routine.
'A lot of work' means it requires much effort and attention every day.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Looking after a puppy means feeding it, walking it, and cleaning up after it every single day. Do you think children should have pets? What might a child learn about life from caring for an animal?
